title: Carbeth Plants 3 x Peony Coral Sunset Paeonia Spring Bare Roots - Orange Coral Hardy Fragrant Spring Summer Flowering Perennial - Easy to Grow UK Garden Favourite - Grow in Borders, Beds and Pots
features: PAEONIA BARE ROOTS Paeonia, commonly known as Peony, is a classic perennial prized for its large, fragrant, showy blooms that appear in late Spring to early Summer. These bareroot plants grow into robust, bushy clumps with attractive foliage and blooms in shades of pink, red, white or coral depending on variety.

HOW TO PLANT PAEONIA Plant bareroot peonies in Autumn or early Spring in fertile, welldrained soil in a sunny position. Position the eyes (buds) just 23cm below soil level to avoid poor flowering. Space plants around 6090cm apart to allow for mature growth. After planting, water thoroughly to help establish roots. Mulch lightly in Spring to retain moisture and suppress weeds. Avoid planting too deeply, which can inhibit flowering.

PLANTING POSITION These hardy perennials are excellent for flower beds, borders, or mixed perennial planting schemes. Their bold structure and longlasting blooms make them standout features in any garden. Once settled, peonies are remarkably easy to care for. Avoid moving them once planted, as they do not like disturbance.

EASY TO STORE If you are not ready to plant the bare root now, then store them in a cool, dry, frost free, well ventilated location. A garage, shed, loft or cupboard are ideal. Open the paper bag to allow air to circulate round but leave the bulbs in their bags until ready to plant.

CARBETH PLANTS We are a Scottish based company with over 20 years experience in horticulture. With a large variety of plants for the garden, indoors and also gift wrapped for special occasions. We pride ourselves on prompt delivery as well as our quality and affordable plants.
description: Bring timeless elegance to your garden with Paeonia bare root plants, commonly known as peonies. These hardy perennials produce large, fragrant, ruffled blooms in a variety of pinks, whites, reds, and corals, depending on the variety supplied. Flowering from late Spring to early Summer, they create an eye-catching display in borders, beds, or mixed perennial schemes and are ideal for cottage gardens. Peonies thrive in full sun and well-drained, fertile soil. Once established, they require minimal care and will return year after year with increasingly abundant blooms. Each bare root will grow into a sturdy, bushy plant with glossy foliage that adds texture even after flowering has finished. Peonies also make exceptional cut flowers. Harvest the blooms when buds are just starting to open to enjoy their scent and beauty indoors. With their long life and classic charm, these plants are a must-have for any gardener looking to create a stunning and reliable seasonal display.
